prompt:
Scene:
A square commercial presentation with realistic materials, clean staging, and believable product photography or retail lighting. Build the frame around the system reviewed on a designer’s table with believable supporting objects. Keep the lighting, camera distance, and spatial organization tuned for a high-signal research image rather than a casual snapshot. The image belongs to the Product Packaging Studio Mockup theme and should feel immediately legible as a prompt atlas theme for product packaging studio mockup, testing photorealism, physics-materials, editing-workflow, commercial-mockup with gpt image 2..

Subject:
Center the study on the system reviewed on a designer’s table with believable supporting objects. Translate the theme into a concrete visual statement instead of an abstract mood board: the viewer should understand what kind of image this is, what is being tested, and why the composition is difficult. Use the slug idea of product packaging studio mockup as a literal guide for subject matter, materials, and staging. If helpful, borrow the broad visual cues suggested by studio, but keep the result original and specific to this study.

Important details:
The mockup should feel saleable and design-ready: sharp edges, controlled reflections, credible paper or plastic behavior, and precise placement. Preserve a disciplined square composition, clear focal hierarchy, and enough secondary information to make the image feel complete rather than underspecified. The image should look polished, novel, and exhibition-ready, with topic-specific credibility coming from the way surfaces, structure, and visual language reinforce Product Packaging Studio Mockup. Avoid generic filler elements that do not serve the main concept.

Use case:
Product Packaging Studio Mockup single study 3/8, testing photorealism, physics-materials, editing-workflow, commercial-mockup through the desk review variation.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, no real brand names, no cheap stock-photo styling, no broken perspective, no watermark, no accidental readable packaging copy, no cluttered retail shelf unless intended.
